2605.00358.title: From Backward Spreading to Forward Replay: Revisiting Target Construction in LLM Parameter Editing
2605.00358.paper_summary: LLM parameter editing methods commonly rely on computing an ideal target hidden-state at a target layer (referred as anchor point) and distributing the target vector to multiple preceding layers (commonly known as backward spreading) for cooperative editing. Although widely used for a long time, its underlying basis have not been systematically investigated. In this paper, we first conduct a systematic study of its foundations, which helps clarify its capability boundaries, practical considerations, and potential failure modes. Then, we propose a simple and elegant alternative that replaces backward spreading with forward-propagation. Instead of optimizing the target at the last editing layer, we optimize the anchor point at the first editing layer, and then propagate it forward to obtain accurate and mutually compatible target hidden-states for all subsequent editing layers. This approach achieves the same computational complexity as existing methods while producing more accurate layer-wise targets. Our method is simple, without interfering with either the computation of the initial target hidden state or any other components of the subsequent editing pipeline, and thus constituting a benefit for a wide range of LLM parameter editing methods.
2605.00358.paper_summary_zh: LLM 參數編輯方法通常依賴於在目標層計算理想的目標隱藏狀態（稱為錨點），並將目標向量分配到多個前面的層（通常稱為向後擴散）以進行協同編輯。儘管長期以來被廣泛使用，但其基本原理尚未得到系統性的研究。在本文中，我們首先對其基礎進行系統研究，這有助於澄清其能力邊界、實際考量和潛在失效模式。然後，我們提出一種簡單而優雅的替代方案，將向後擴散替換為向前傳播。我們不是在最後的編輯層優化目標，而是在第一個編輯層優化錨點，然後向前傳播以獲得準確且相互兼容的目標隱藏狀態，用於所有後續的編輯層。這種方法在計算複雜度上與現有方法相同，同時產生更準確的層級目標。我們的方法簡單，不會干擾初始目標隱藏狀態的計算或後續編輯流程中的任何其他組件，因此對於廣泛的 LLM 參數編輯方法構成了一種好處。
2605.00358.author: Wei Liu et.al.
2605.00358.authors: Wei Liu, Hongkai Liu, Zhiying Deng, Yee Whye Teh, Wee Sun Lee
